title: Field Lines, Repetition, Combination, Structured Fields, and Limits · Execution Trace: Field Resource Disclosure
summary: A parser stores one value per name, splits every comma, accepts obsolete folding, merges Set-Cookie, and treats all field values as trusted UTF-8 text. Planned artifact: Add and verify the field resource disclosure increment in loss-aware bounded field-section model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound field bytes and count, reject obsolete folding and controls, redact secrets, and preserve only safe diagnostic evidence.
Parse RFC 9651 items, lists, dictionaries, parameters, numbers, strings, tokens, bytes, booleans, dates, and display strings strictly when a field opts in.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Field Resource Disclosure · Theory Field Resource Disclosure
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 2256 in a archival client parsing incomplete close-delimited responses. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve repeated field lines and reject sections beyond a bounded policy; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
